evidence: The candidate manifest is 64 LF-terminated rows with 64 unique safe relative paths; its SHA-256 is 213026f36faa56c7ee397c029274102265a04941309fea60dcd21f8d14096ce8 and sha256sum -c passed 64/64.

The reviewed paths exactly equal the union of scripts.hpc.prepare_research_run.RQ014_REVIEW_REQUIRED_PATHS (53 paths) and the inherited v1.3 checksum set (14 paths), yielding 64 unique union members with no missing or extra path.
The set retains all round-1 through round-4 blocked manifests, both role reviews, and remediation records, plus the exact F01-F04 forensics evidence byte.
findings.summary: The fifth-round review manifest is complete, exact, and preserves inherited and prior-round provenance.

evidence: The frozen clean bootstrap opens and locks fd8, opens the exact managed wrapper as fd9, hashes /proc/$$/fd/9, and executes /bin/sh on that same descriptor; the wrapper contains no fd8/fd9 fallback and rechecks readlink, regular-file status, and descriptor/path device-inode-mode identity.

scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py verifies the fixed fd8/fd9 capability at lines 29-68 before the materializer/preflight loader at lines 97-125, then carries only the module identity token into the CLI and submission gates.

The hostile copied-launcher probe with --rq014-only and missing capability failed before either dependency marker executed. Additional direct validate and direct --submit probes with --rq014-only omitted both failed with 'RQ014 schema v2 requires the managed RQ014-only wrapper' and neither imported the hostile materializer or preflight.

_validate_cli_entry_mode and prepare_and_submit reject False, True, and arbitrary caller objects; the focused suite also covers missing, wrong-target, wrong-inode, symlink, directory, positive inherited-fd, fixed-base, and cross-label cases.
findings.summary: The clean-bootstrap descriptor capability closes the direct flag, validate, submit, and pre-import bypasses within its declared local provenance boundary.

evidence: Formal review validation requires exact schema, role, manifest binding, NO_BLOCKER, zero unresolved counts, fresh/no-rating/no-HPC attestations, valid findings, and distinct nonempty reviewer_agent identities.

G0 validation binds and machine-checks the exact F01-F04 evidence path/hash and closure fields; the v1.5 registry legally represents the PI-waived surfaces as INACCESSIBLE rather than negative findings.

The final bundle must include every reviewed digest plus formal G1, both exact reviewer artifacts, and the exact review manifest; execution uses only bundle-registered regular blobs from the declared published commit and rechecks snapshot/bundle bytes.

Current central authorization permits only rq014_g2_declassification_export. Preflight is receipt-conditional in the execution contract but centrally denied, and all rating-bearing/resource/replay operations remain denied until a new authority refreeze, two reviews, formal G1, and final bundle.
findings.summary: G0, dual-review G1, final-bundle closure, commit snapshotting, and staged authorization are fail closed.

evidence: Production run specs are direct-child read-only regular non-symlink canonical JSON read once through O_NOFOLLOW; retained bytes are used for validation, sealing, and submission without reopening the path.

Every declassification source is opened once with O_NOFOLLOW, retained, checked for regular-file identity continuity, exact size and SHA-256, parsed only from those retained bytes, and receipted with that same digest.

The generated job and sbatch command both use --export=NIL, managed Python uses -I -S -B -X utf8, exact materializer and preflight imports precede the fixed entrypoint under empty package paths, and stdlib/native closure checks precede managed execution.

Pre-submit failures remove the partial run root and write retryable FAILED evidence; any failure after sbatch starts retains raw response and known job identity when available and records non-retryable SUBMISSION_STATE_UNKNOWN.
findings.summary: Source/spec one-read retention, exact imports, NIL isolation, preflight refreeze, and rollback semantics remain coherent.

evidence: The execution contract and PI decision explicitly state that the descriptor capability is a local provenance check, not a cryptographic secret against deliberate same-account descriptor emulation.

Within that boundary the exact wrapper digest remains the clean-bootstrap trust anchor, while ordinary direct flags and caller-supplied booleans or objects fail closed; no broader multi-user or hostile-account security claim is made.
findings.summary: Same-account deliberate descriptor emulation remains an honestly bounded non-cryptographic residual, not an unclosed execution-contract claim.

evidence: Using the available project verifier interpreter, the frozen eight-file focused command completed with 194 passed in 15.79 seconds; no rating data, raw TFRecord, HPC connection, production run root, sbatch, or Slurm operation was used.

Independent static checks passed for all 15 manifest Python ASTs, all 20 manifest JSON files with duplicate-key and non-finite rejection, six shell/sbatch syntax targets, git diff --check, and a repeated exact 64-file digest verification.
findings.summary: Safe local dynamic and static verification found no unresolved execution or governance defect.
